video_subject: Forex Market: Dollar Strength Amid Trump Pressure
video_script: The US dollar is holding firmer than almost anyone expected this June. The dollar index is hovering near 99.6, up on the month, even as President Trump keeps publicly pushing for lower interest rates. Here's the tension driving the market: Trump wants a weaker dollar to boost American exports. But his own playbook of tariffs and tax cuts tends to do the opposite, pushing the greenback higher. Add sticky inflation at 3.8 percent and an unsigned US-Iran ceasefire, and traders are still leaning on the dollar as a safe haven. Now all eyes turn to the Federal Reserve. New Chair Kevin Warsh has just taken over, and his first meeting could set the tone for the dollar through the rest of 2026, with markets not even ruling out a rate hike. For the forex market, one thing is clear: the next big move in the dollar may come down to what Washington does next.
